explanation: The original command is already a valid true positive for the Sigma rule. No mutation is needed to trigger the rule.
Inserted quotes around the command to test if the Sigma rule is affected by command-line formatting.
Substituted '-Command' with '-c', which is an equivalent short option in PowerShell.
Omitted the '-Command' flag, which is sometimes optional in PowerShell when the command is provided directly.
Used backticks to escape the command, testing if the Sigma rule is affected by escape characters.
Appended a harmless pipeline to test if the Sigma rule is affected by additional command-line arguments.
Inserted an environment variable setting before the main command to test if the Sigma rule is affected by additional logic in the command.
Modified the command to include a common PowerShell output redirection to test if the Sigma rule is affected by such changes.
Added a common PowerShell flag '-NoProfile' to test if the Sigma rule is affected by additional flags.
Added a common PowerShell flag '-ExecutionPolicy Bypass' to test if the Sigma rule is affected by additional flags.
